frameworks.name: BOLD-fMRI hemodynamic response
frameworks.one_line: Hemodynamic proxy of neural activity measured as a contrast response function

frameworks.name: MR spectroscopy of neurotransmitters
frameworks.one_line: Single-voxel proton MRS quantifying glutamate and GABA concentrations in V1

frameworks.name: natural image statistics
frameworks.one_line: Low-contrast prevalence in natural vision frames the observed neurochemical range

core_question: How do neurochemical (glutamate, GABA) and BOLD responses in human V1 scale with image contrast?
key_assumptions: single-voxel MRS in V1 indexes local glutamate and GABA pools
BOLD contrast response indexes contrast-driven neural activity
block-design contrast presentations elicit measurable steady-state responses
manipulations: block presentation of stimuli during simultaneous BOLD-fMRI and MRS at 7T
key_findings: BOLD signal in V1 scaled linearly with image contrast
glutamate concentration scaled linearly with contrast but reached significance only at highest contrast
GABA concentration remained steady across all contrast levels
interpretations: neurochemical concentrations are maintained at lower contrast ranges matching natural-vision statistics
high stimulus intensity is critical to detect visually modulated glutamate via MRS
limitations_noted: glutamate change detectable only at the highest contrast suggests limited MRS sensitivity
connecting_signals: natural image statistics and visual cortex
